_doc: WorldSpec — the semantic design of a world, authored by an AI World Director
and compiled deterministically by the Godot generator (see
docs/AI_WORLD_AUTHORING.md). This is NOT a list of trees/rocks: it names
regions, pins a few landmark anchors, and declares relationships the
validator (tools/worldc.gd) checks. Everything else stays procedural.
Coordinates are in CHUNK space (1 chunk = 16 tiles), so the layout is
seed-independent and reproducible. regions[].shape is a disc {center,radius}
that forces a parent biome + a named zone with an entry-level 'req'; your
sub-biomes (groves, bamboo, dense_forest...) still stamp inside them.
anchors pin a POI (from data/world/pois.json) to one chunk and may pin a
named boss from data/enemies.json. To expand the world: add a region, drop
an anchor at its center, then run `worldc --validate`.
name: The Eastvale Realm

regions.name: Eastvale Commons
regions.motif: Gentle rolling commons around the home camp — the beginner heartland (Lumbridge vibes): open sightlines, a few oaks, easy paths out to every neighbour.
regions.silhouette: low hedgerows and a lone campfire

regions.name: Kingsreach
regions.motif: The capital on the northern downs — the Crown Obelisk spire is visible from far off and every service in the realm is gathered here.
regions.silhouette: the Crown Obelisk above the rooftops

regions.name: Goldfurrow Fields
regions.motif: Endless golden wheat between Eastvale and the capital — the realm's granary, swaying amber under the sun.

regions.name: Oakmantle Woods
regions.motif: Deep broadleaf oak forest west of home — dense groves, woodcutting country, and a burrow that drops into the Molehollow.

regions.name: Pinscar Deepwood
regions.motif: Cold pine highlands to the northwest — straight black pines, needle floor, and a hush that gets heavier the deeper you go.

regions.name: Grimwarden Ruins
regions.motif: A shattered keep on the high rocks due north — the Broken Spire leans over a toppled shrine, and the beastmaster Amaruq stalks the rubble.

regions.name: The Dreadmoor
regions.motif: The southern wilderness — a drowned, fog-bound moor with few safe ways in, where players fight players. High risk, high reward.

relationships.why: The beginner home must have a bank at the camp.
The capital is the realm's full-service hub.
Smithing belongs in the capital forge.
Players must be able to walk from home to the capital.
The ruins landmark must be reachable on foot.
The dungeon must be reachable on foot.
The wilderness should have few, memorable ways in.
